name: Sandra Neguinha De Pina
role: Graduation · June 10, 2026 · 20 guests · East Providence RI

handling.tone: Cape Verdean / Lusophone heritage. Open in English; if she replies in Portuguese, switch. The 'Neguinha' nickname suggests warmth and informality is the right register.
handling.voice_adjustments: Andre voice: warm, bilingual-aware, brief. The Portuguese-template auto-fire on Apr 24 may have already hit her — be careful next move.
handling.context: Small graduation lead, June 10. 20 guests = drop-off territory ($660 ballpark). No response to any outreach. Cape Verdean / Portuguese-Lusophone surname pattern + East Providence demographic = cultural fit, modest scale.

context_anchors: Cape Verdean / Lusophone heritage (De Pina, Neguinha)
East Providence RI, 02915 — Cape Verdean/Portuguese corridor
June 10 (Wed) — possibly school graduation, weekday celebration

lead.source: FB Lead Form — VIDEO RODS WEDDING & FACELESS
lead.next_move: One bilingual-aware re-engagement message: drop-off vs light buffet + cuisine direction

enrichment.identity_notes: 'De Pina' = Cape Verdean / Portuguese-Lusophone surname. 'Neguinha' = Portuguese affectionate form, common in Brazilian and Cape Verdean communities. East Providence has meaningful Cape Verdean and Portuguese community.
win_angles: Lusophone cultural fit — open EN, switch PT if she does
Right-size the offer — $660 drop-off is realistic
Cultural cuisine option — Brazilian/Portuguese-leaning would feel familiar
Graduation = emotional milestone, smaller event with real meaning
nepq_openers: Bilingual: 'Hey Sandra — for graduation June 10 with 20 guests, drop-off and you handle it, or light setup with attendant? Cuisine — Brazilian/Portuguese-leaning, or open? Tudo bem se preferir português.'
Frame: 'Whose graduation? Want to make sure the food honors the moment for them.'
Anchor: 'For 20 people, drop-off ~$X, light buffet ~$Y. Quick comparison?'
enrichment.strategic_note: Small but culturally-aligned. One warm bilingual-aware message that gets cuisine direction + service style answered, then fast simple quote. Don't overinvest. If she comes back, close fast. If not, accept the loss — small jobs need fast wins, not long courtships.

notes: Cape Verdean cultural fit — language register matters

Drop to long-term followup if no response in 7 days
